description: Manual acceptance coverage map for the maintained Playwright E2E gate. Each entry links a canonical acceptance flow to automated coverage, intentional manual coverage, or a deferred issue.

entries.acceptance: Shared design-system tokens and core primitive contracts

coverage.notes: Covers the canonical token inventory, document and Shadow DOM scopes, direct panel and Gallery imports, removal of Gallery-only aliases and global legacy controls, web-accessible token loading, and preservation of existing panel layout contracts.

Covers typed plain-DOM Button, IconButton, Input, Select, Toggle, Badge, StatusPill, Kbd, Toast, Card, and SectionHeader output; native semantics and callbacks; pressed, waiting, disabled, invalid, semantic, privacy-safe, focus, packaging, and reduced-motion contracts.
Covers critique-ready overview, interactive, disabled, waiting, semantic feedback, narrow, and reduced-motion previews with keyboard-native control interactions and action assertions.

coverage.reason: Token and primitive styling requires a packaged-extension visual and keyboard smoke check across the injected Shadow DOM.
coverage.notes: Load the built extension, open the panel over the single-image fixture, and inspect the core-primitives Storybook stories. Verify the panel and Gallery retain their prior rendering, every primitive matches the canonical hierarchy, keyboard focus is visible, disabled and waiting states remain distinct, narrow content wraps without overflow, reduced motion removes animation without hiding state, and no stylesheet request fails.

entries.acceptance: Shared design-system panel shell and primary workflow

coverage.notes: Covers shared header, status pill, toast, minimized and detached chrome; always-visible navigation, capture, slideshow, and Grab Mode controls; state-owned More controls; typed URL editor Apply action; Host target primitives; focus-safe native controls; privacy-safe status text and titles; section detach placement; narrow layouts; reduced motion; and stylesheet packaging.
Covers the 420px attached shell plus minimized, detached, waiting, error, privacy, expanded workflow, Grab Mode, narrow, and reduced-motion presentation states.

Covers packaged-extension open/minimize/expand/close, approved standard and narrow panel geometry, deterministic handoff artifacts, capture feedback, grouped Settings and Help surfaces, Host selection, URL application, visible navigation and automation actions, More controls, detached windows, status refresh, and focus/scroll-preserving rerenders.

coverage.reason: The handoff calls for visual fidelity over an arbitrary host page and browser-level reduced-motion behavior.
coverage.notes: Load the packaged extension over the single-image fixture at 420px and a narrow viewport. Compare against the handoff panel and capture-flash references; verify hierarchy, density, glows, typography, status/toast motion, header drag, minimize, detach/restore, keyboard focus, privacy masking, and no failed stylesheet requests.

entries.acceptance: React workspace shade, floating windows, four edge rails, and private versioned restoration

coverage.notes: Covers v2 schema sanitization and legacy migration, install-secret HMAC keys without raw URL persistence, one placement registry, panel and section restoration, shade/snap/unsnap/reorder transitions, pointer thresholds, viewport clamping and rail fallback, React DOM adoption, focus, and scroll preservation.
Covers the handoff-sized left and top rail compositions, ordered cards, a floating window, React action dispatch, and representative Queue, Recents, and Controls bodies.

Covers packaged four-edge snapping, pointer and keyboard previews, shade versus attached collapse, button and threshold-drag unsnap with cancel, rail order, extension-surface panel collision resolution, responsive rail fallback and clamping, focus handoff, deterministic floating/shaded/railed/restored artifacts, private persisted restore and reset across extension restart, local build-overlay stacking, and the overlay-only boundary across responsive, fixed/sticky, feed, nested-scroll, RTL, iframe, transformed-root, SPA, navigation, fullscreen, zoom-equivalent, narrow, reduced-motion, coarse-pointer, interrupted-drag, and exact host-baseline cases.

coverage.reason: The arbitrary-host overlay, browser zoom clamp, and final handoff visual fidelity need packaged-extension inspection.
coverage.notes: Follow the canonical Workspace Rails Cross-Site Safety wiki script at 1440x900 and 200% zoom. Confirm rail width 344px, top and bottom height 240px, supported overlay and fallback behavior, exact host cleanup, keyboard and pointer escape paths, private persistence/reset, Recall remaining panel-relative, and chrome matching handoff screenshot 11.

entries.acceptance: Shared RecordRow across Queue, Recents, Recall, and Gallery

coverage.notes: Covers the canonical thumbnail, source/name/meta hierarchy, selected/locked/key-unavailable/privacy states, stored-original indicator, native Gallery button semantics, shared adoption across all four record surfaces, existing row interactions, and stylesheet packaging.
Covers default, selected and captured, locked, key-unavailable, privacy-masked, missing-thumbnail, narrow, keyboard, and action states.
Covers packaged Queue, Recents, and Recall row selection, preview, paging, capture, durable state, and lifecycle behavior after migration to the shared primitive.

coverage.reason: The handoff defines a signature thumbnail bleed, fade, glow, and state hierarchy that requires visual comparison in the injected panel and extension Gallery.
coverage.notes: Load the packaged extension and compare Queue, Recents, Recall, and Gallery against the handoff. Verify full-height faded thumbnails, source/name/meta hierarchy, selected dominance over the stored-original dot, locked and key-unavailable dimming, privacy masking, missing thumbnails, keyboard focus, narrow widths, and no layout flicker.

entries.acceptance: Shared design-system Settings, integrations, shortcuts, and Help surfaces

coverage.notes: Covers grouped Settings composition, native control preservation, primitive variants, input/select/toggle treatment, encryption badges, provider status, file pickers, danger actions, Help keycaps, privacy-inert copy, narrow reflow, focus, disabled, waiting, error, and reduced-motion contracts.
Covers standard and narrow Settings groups plus locked, unlocked, waiting, error, connected-provider, restore-preview, destructive, shortcut, and Help states using production view factories.

Covers packaged Settings open and interaction behavior, privacy and build-overlay settings, import/export and encrypted backup states, detach/restore, detached scrolling, focus preservation, and state changes routed through existing controllers.

coverage.reason: The handoff specifies dense visual hierarchy and state contrast across browser-controlled form elements and detached extension windows.
coverage.notes: Load the packaged extension at standard and narrow widths; inspect attached and detached Settings plus Help. Verify grouped hierarchy, sticky headings, native input/select/file controls, focus, locked and privacy states, connected/waiting/error statuses, destructive separation, reduced motion, preserved scroll, and no stylesheet request failures.

entries.acceptance: Shared design-system Gallery and final high-fidelity acceptance

coverage.notes: Covers Gallery composition with shared Button, IconButton, Input, Select, Badge, Card, SectionHeader, StatusPill, and RecordRow primitives; canonical tokens without compatibility aliases; native form semantics; paging, AND-composed source/kind/type filters, active-filter summary and clear behavior, search, album, locked/privacy, distinct empty/no-match, loading, error, focus restoration, and independent per-card album states.
Covers populated, interactive search/filter/clear controls, locked/private, empty, loading, error, narrow, and reduced-motion Gallery states using the production view factory.

Covers the packaged extension Gallery with real durable IndexedDB records, shared primitive adoption, canonical queue order, debounced search, source-host/image-type/record-kind filters with AND behavior, active-filter clear and no-match state, album creation and membership, native keyboard order with focus preserved through filter and background refresh renders, narrow overflow, and reduced-motion behavior; verifies browsing, search, filtering, and album membership do not reorder the durable queue.

coverage.reason: Browser zoom, drag-and-drop affordances, and final visual comparison against the supplied mocked-tab reference require a packaged-extension inspection.
coverage.notes: Open Gallery from the packaged panel and compare its dark extension-page shell, hierarchy, density, type, borders, glows, status, cards, and controls with the mocked-tab handoff. Exercise search plus source/kind/type filters, filter clearing, privacy mode, 200% zoom, keyboard-only navigation, album drag/drop, locked/private records, captured-original indicators, empty/no-match and load-error states, reduced motion, and confirm no context rail, destination navigation, Recents, or failed stylesheet requests appear.

entries.acceptance: M08 automation, keybindings, and request governance

coverage.notes: Covers the canonical bare-key table, typing/modifier/native-row exclusions, Trail and Grab routing, capture/pin semantics, 1400ms repeated-key reset, Controls hint reset, privacy-safe feedback, Help/Settings/Escape transitions, Down-arrow assignment and existing download path including imported data images, cross-tab setting reload, source-page reload, extension process restart, duplicate-listener prevention, fixed-viewport capture/Help artifacts, slideshow controls, opposite-direction manual interruption, retry reachability, and request-governor capping.

Covers Chromium command registration for the build-info overlay toggle and assignable Image Trail action commands via chrome.commands.getAll, hidden-panel browser shortcut no-op behavior, plus the Settings checkbox that hides/shows the overlay and verifies selectable overlay values without blocking outside-page clicks.
Covers manifest command metadata for the browser action shortcut entry, assignable Image Trail action commands, and the suggested Alt/Option+Shift+B binding for the build-info overlay toggle.

Covers the exact canonical registry, case-insensitive bare letters, handler-declined Down behavior, all excluded targets/modifiers, capture/pin/download routing and locked fallback, real data-image URL resolution, privacy-safe copy, Help grouping, Settings assignment, schema migration, queue repaging after external queue-view settings, typed cross-tab notification with no-listener resilience, 1400ms repeated-key reset, render survival, and teardown cleanup.
Covers grouped Help, the assignable Down control at standard and narrow widths, interaction dispatch, row overflow, and persistence through the production React Settings destination.

coverage.reason: Final handoff fidelity and browser-owned shortcut assignment require a packaged-extension inspection.
coverage.notes: Compare the capture feedback, Help, and Settings Automation surfaces with handoff screenshots 03, 10, and 13. Exercise every canonical bare key using keyboard-only navigation, repeat a shortcut before 1400ms, switch the Down assignment across two source tabs, reload a source tab, restart the extension process, inspect browser extension shortcuts, verify reduced motion, and confirm capture/download feedback contains no image metadata or URL and opens no unintended tab.

Covers #373 bounded manual navigation (latest wins): a rapid press burst coalesces into one net jump, cancelQueuedManualNavigation drops queued presses on Escape/Stop, a long governor wait abandons manual-only intent with a visible message, parsed-field +/- bursts fold into one net transform (a +/- pair loads nothing, interleaved interactions preserve order), the navigationBusy waiting state toggles around the drain, and an unchanged status toast is not rebuilt across renders so stale messages cannot replay their enter animation. Buffered prewarming responsiveness: a blocked seek probes the skip-budget frontier concurrently and lands past a failed run in one step, and the GET the user is blocked on bypasses the prefetch concurrency cap.

Covers service-worker/id resolution, programmatic injection with a test-build grant limited to the local fixture origin, panel open/close, single-image auto-select, multi-image target picking, minimize/expand/close/reopen lifecycle, duplicate-DOM prevention, and close cleanup. The production activeTab toolbar gesture and optional prompt remain manual browser checks.
Covers removal of static all-sites injection and required host access, retention of activeTab plus scripting, optional http/https declarations, and exact per-origin permission requests.

Covers the panel open-gallery action, runtime message classification, bounded gallery paging controls, URL/data/blob activation decisions, and locked private-record disabling without implementing search, filters, or albums.
Covers the packaged extension Gallery with durable records, shared design-system controls, canonical ordering, search, album membership, narrow layout, keyboard order, and reduced motion.

coverage.reason: The production toolbar gesture, cross-extension-tab focus, encrypted-original preview tab, and final visual comparison remain browser-level checks.
coverage.notes: Load the built extension, open the Queue menu, choose Open gallery, verify a dedicated extension tab lists only durable pins/bookmarks, URL-only rows open their saved URL, unlocked captured originals use encrypted preview, locked originals stay disabled, and Queue/Recents/Recall/pCloud/import-export state does not change while browsing.

Covers gallery metadata matching, privacy-mode search masking, matched-result queue order, single-pass durable search loading, zero-as-unlimited gallery limit migration, draft search input handling, and the gallery search/page-limit controls.
Covers debounced search and clear behavior over real durable IndexedDB records while preserving the canonical queue order.

coverage.reason: The complete metadata matrix, privacy-mode term masking, and unlimited-result visual review remain manual browser checks.
coverage.notes: Load the built extension, open the gallery, save a finite gallery page limit and then 0, type host/filename/label/extension queries, verify search waits for a typing pause, results stay in queue order, no-match and cleared states are explicit, privacy mode hides URL/label matches, thumbnails crop from the top, and queue/Recents/Recall/pCloud/import-export state does not change.

Covers durable album and membership stores, v8 IndexedDB migration/indexes, idempotent ordered membership add/remove without queueUpdatedAt changes, album and bookmark mutation notifications, gallery debounced refresh hooks, privacy-safe notification payloads, album message registry fallbacks, gallery album create/select/rename/delete controls, custom add-to-album popovers with independent per-card choices, add/remove membership actions, selected-album missing-record status, v2 full-backup album metadata export/import including album-only backups, duplicate UUID remapping, and explicit skipped membership reporting.
Covers album creation, selection, per-card membership, selected-album filtering, and explicit verification that membership changes do not mutate durable queue order.

coverage.reason: Rename/delete, drag-and-drop, concurrent external refresh, missing-membership recovery, and full-backup restore remain manual browser checks.
coverage.notes: Load the built extension, create captured and uncaptured durable records, open Gallery, pin/capture from another tab and verify the Gallery updates without browser refresh, create/rename/delete an album, add/remove records through the card popover, choose albums for several cards and apply one to verify other pending choices remain, drag a card onto an album chip, search from the header inside the selected album, verify missing/deleted referenced records are omitted with status text, confirm the gallery uses the dark panel palette and privacy mode still masks URL-derived record metadata, verify queue/Recall/Recents order is unchanged, then run pCloud/full-backup restore and verify albums restore without duplicating encrypted originals.

Covers projection ownership plus row interaction dispatch: plain click selects once, selected-row click or focused-row Enter projects through capture/preview, Space keeps selection-first activation, arrows move single selection/focus across History/Queue/Recall shadow roots, locked rows stay disabled, and private placeholder preview is blocked before preload/fetch.

Covers Recent preview projection into the selected host image, #390 focused-row click then ArrowDown then Enter loading, failed projection preserving the previous successful image, same-current already-projected reporting, and release/close restoration of host image src/srcset/sizes and extension-owned styles.

Covers #392 session-only recovery when a redraw-heavy page replaces the selected image node: the active projection, responsive-attribute clearing, selected styling, and handle are restored before the next animation frame; release restores the replacement node's host-owned state, and changed-content replacements are rejected.
Covers #429: a successful record preview clears a stale failed draftUrl so the URL editor and parsed fields rebuild from the projected URL.
Covers #429 end to end: ok load, 404 load (draft parks on the dead URL), double-click a recent — the projection lands and the URL editor shows the projected record's URL, not the failed draft.

Covers #434: plain-URL record previews load through the applySelectedUrl pipeline (record-preview session, projection order, field-state persistence) and reset stale failed/active field markers and the failed draft from the previous URL.

Covers #434 end to end: step a field into a mocked 404 (field row shows the failed marker), double-click an earlier recent — the projection lands, the URL editor follows the record, and no field renders the failed marker.

Covers #447: a Next step that skips a dead run of failed neighbors and then stops leaves no stray red failed-field outline on the field, which rests on its last-good value. The quiet-skip failure marker is transient and reconciled when the navigation drain comes to rest.

Covers #450: a load failure always sets failedFieldId (functional re-basing + review record) and records the failure, but the visible feedback is gated by the loadFailureFeedback mode — the field ring renders only when showFieldFailure is not Mute, and the projection failure branch flashes the status error only in Alert (Display/Mute keep the prior status/message).

Covers #450 end to end: in Mute (default) a failed apply surfaces no red field ring and no error status, while the same failure in Alert flashes the red status + HTTP error — proving Mute suppresses real feedback rather than being a no-op.
Covers #425: the recents list participates in the render scroll snapshot, so selecting a row (which rerenders the panel) keeps the list's scroll position.

Covers #367: the attached Settings section is a layout-reserved scroll region, so expanding/collapsing settings groups keeps the clicked summary and the sections below Settings stationary, and the region's scroll position survives the rerender caused by applying a setting.
Covers open/close, duplicate panel root behavior, minimize/expand, close/reopen, and single-image auto-select selected-target preview assertions.

Covers the Recall drawer's inline width staying clamped inside the viewport's edge padding on a narrow viewport, mirroring the CSS max-width so the Close control can't be pushed off-screen (regression: a stale 240px width floor). Header follow-up to #408: the detach control and Show/Hide hint form fixed right-hand columns (single auto margin; fixed-width hint), and Reset all availability compares resolved image URLs so a restored baseline no longer shows it when nothing changed.

entries.acceptance: Target picker captures only the selected image

coverage.notes: Covers multi-image target picking and the visible selected-target indicator on only the chosen host image.

Covers parsed-field state sync plus the serializable Field Editor view model and typed design-system FieldRow: active position/count and collapsed hierarchy, native control semantics and keyboard order, state priority, previous/next field selection, status flags/chips, navigation eligibility, transform/reset availability, template-filtered and invalid-URL empty states, muted failure visibility, JSON serialization, split-child routing, commit-before-command ordering, numeric display modes, baseline restoration, privacy masking without label/value/title leakage, the user-facing Field Editor section name (#248), critique coverage (#249), and bounded saved stepping presets whose exact unsplit numeric fields are reviewable before persistence through the existing URL template store (#207).

Covers URL editor apply, parsed numeric field increment/decrement, failed parsed-field loads without Recent rows, included-field Previous/Next navigation (including stepping ALL included fields together into one combined URL, #263), learned template visibility, and close/reopen parsed-field restore for the same selected image context.
Covers passed and failed URL review status records, JSON export/import restore preview, confirm import, and no Recents, pins/bookmarks, Recall, or encrypted-original side effects.

Covers successful image loads adding Recents, failed loads not adding Recents, durable pins persisting across panel close/reopen, Recall loading offscreen queue rows, recalled rows moving to the capped visible queue, Select all scoped to visible Recents/queue/Recall rows, and Clear results hiding Recall rows without deleting durable queue records.

Covers Recents retention plus #452 sparse-row display-mode persistence, Settings UI dispatch, mode-specific Recents row classes, privacy-masked Recents labels, the CSS contract for adaptive/full/half/compact row sizing, and #478 centered Adaptive metadata at three or more rows while one or two rows remain top-left and two-row thumbnails retain the full-width background effect with a fully visible center 40% and symmetric edge feathering.

Covers visible Recents being populated only by successful image loads, failed loads being excluded from the transient recent list, Visible recents limiting, Max kept recents bounding hidden session retention, Keep hidden this session retention, and Show hidden recents reveal behavior.

Covers saved-row lookup messages, plain/protected URL lookup without queue-order mutation, recent enrichment from off-page saved rows, duplicate pin prevention, capture skip/update behavior, post-capture blob cleanup on failed durable save, linked recent cleanup, and Delete original resolving non-visible durable rows.

Covers a captured queue row moving off the visible page into Recall, then reloading the same URL into Recents with the pinned/captured state and durable actions reflected without creating duplicate controls.
Covers selection state plus row-level Cmd/Ctrl toggle and Shift range behavior for Recents, Queue, and Recall without triggering projection.
Covers current selected host-image export fallback, shifted Save As metadata, and selected Recents, visible queue, and Recall image export order.
Covers encrypted image export/import plus locked or unavailable encrypted rows remaining disabled until keys are available.
Covers encrypted image JSON export, clean imported-image restore after unlock, and wrong-key or wrong-import-type failures without adding plaintext rows.

entries.acceptance: Local original capture survives remote loss

coverage.notes: Covers selected durable-pin repair: existing encrypted blob references are verified through key-only IndexedDB reads without hydrating encrypted bytes, missing or metadata-only originals are re-captured through the existing capture path, private rows without a source URL are rejected, permission-needed capture pauses the batch, and queue timestamps remain unchanged.
Covers encrypted original setup, capture from a queue row, stored-original indicator, and selected-record export preferring stored original bytes when unlocked.

Covers oversized capture refusal from a pinned queue row against a generated >25 MB fixture: the failure status names the size limit, no stored-original indicator appears, the pinned record stays listed, and the Storage health originals row keeps its pre-attempt count and byte total.

Covers key backup export, wrong-password fail-closed import, key backup restore, and unlocking captured originals after key restore without exposing the encryption password or raw CryptoKey material in the backup JSON.
Covers the extracted Settings group views and representative dispatch behavior without changing the full Settings layout contract.

Covers Settings-only reachability for Encrypted originals, Image utilities, Import / Export, and pCloud, utility-section open/closed persistence across settings rerenders, hiding utilities when Settings closes, and history/bookmark export/import preview access.

Covers #470 group ordering/open-state orchestration plus focused Display, Privacy, Automation, Utilities, and System DOM/event contracts, including bounded single dispatch, metadata preservation, preload command ordering, storage rendering, and confirmed deletion.

Covers provider operations, trusted and sanitized bounded backup-history persistence, disconnected-state hydration, verification-method rendering, plus the narrow optional https://*.pcloud.com/* request and explicit denial result before OAuth.
Covers multi-entry backup history with downloaded-byte and provider-checksum verification details in the live pCloud utility layout.

coverage.reason: Live pCloud permission approval, OAuth, and backup/restore proof requires user-controlled provider credentials.

coverage.notes: Covers mocked pCloud connect, token-hiding UI boundary, manual encrypted backup upload metadata, persistent backup history across disconnect and Settings rerender, restore candidate listing, and restore preview without real pCloud secrets.

coverage.reason: Provider API spike remains an operator-run proof with local OAuth callback and cleanup controls.

coverage.notes: Covers product UI boundary behavior with mocked pCloud provider responses while live provider proof remains intentionally manual.

Includes the parsed-field navigation consecutive-miss short-circuit (#287): a dead run stops after max(3, neighborPreloadRadius) probes, successes reset the consecutive budget, and a 50-skip-per-drain outer net bounds queued bursts.

Covers #447: when the navigation drain stops after skipping (no loadable candidate), the transient quiet-skip failed-field marker and stale failed draft are reconciled at the drain's resting point, so the field is not left flagged failed while showing its last-good value and the next press bases from the good URL.

Covers #450: the 'Skipped a failed image candidate.' toast is failure feedback, so it fires only in Alert mode; Display/Mute skip past the failed neighbor to the next good candidate silently (the skip is driven by the request-policy cache, not the toast).
Browser-level automation and request-governance coverage.

Covers #62 retry-context lifecycle, permission CTA routing, immediate permission-request handoff, grant success through the normal capture completion path, denial retention, dismissal, source deletion before retry, deletion during capture completion, orphan cleanup, and non-permission failures.
CapturePermissionError exposes the origin-specific Grant permission and retry action.

coverage.reason: Live extension permission prompts require a headful browser-chrome check; use tests/e2e/pages/third-party-cdn.html to execute approval and denial against the localhost image origin.

coverage.notes: Request-governance E2E coverage.
Browser-level retry and request-governance flows.

coverage.reason: Critique coverage still requires human review of visual hierarchy, accessibility cues, and interaction feedback.
Visual consistency across dense row states remains human-reviewed.

coverage.notes: Covers #426: previewing/projecting a Recents, queue, or Recall row requires a real double-click — a second click within the 500ms window on the same row previews, while a stale later click re-selects; window semantics unit-tested in the shared registerPreviewRowClick helper. Covers #446 Recents sparse-row sizing by locking the attached list to stable 120px auto rows, start-aligned grid tracks, a one-to-three-row viewport cap, viewport-only user resize, and panel scroll anchoring when sparse Recents grow above visible Queue rows.
Covers #430: the Recents and Queue action toolbars render inside their section header rows (heading | wrapping toolbar | pinned detach column) instead of floating rows below the heading.

Covers #438/#441: the Recents/Queue HEADER ROWS collapse and expand the section body (role=button, aria-expanded, Enter/Space); a collapsed section keeps its header-row actions usable; clicks on interactive children never toggle.

Covers #438/#441 end to end with geometry pins: single-row header (hint shares the row), the far-right hint area and heading side both toggle, header actions stay usable while collapsed (pinning while the queue is collapsed works), and toolbar clicks do not toggle.

Covers #443: collapsing a Recents/Queue section parks its list scrollTop in PanelLayoutState (the list leaves the DOM, so the plain scroll snapshot cannot bridge it) and re-expanding restores the offset; an open list still follows its live snapshot across ordinary re-renders rather than a stale parked value.
Covers #443 end to end with real layout: scroll an overflowing Recents list, Hide then Show, and assert the rebuilt list reopens at the pre-collapse scrollTop instead of jumping to the top.

coverage.reason: Planning baseline review is documentation and behavior-map inspection, not executable product behavior.

coverage.notes: Covers detach/restore reducer idempotence plus the DOM contract: header detach control dispatch, placeholder restore, dialog window rendering with section content, Escape restore, title drag position + layout-state persistence, list-scroll preservation across rerenders, and minimized clearing, plus the #397 window chrome: minimize toggle (collapse, aria-expanded, layout-state persistence across rerenders) and close-to-restore (X). #217 Settings detach: detach control, window/placeholder conditional on the active Settings destination, wider window default, settings-action routing from the window, and the Escape-in-editable-control guard. #215 completion: Queue (bookmarks) detach, drag-out gesture (threshold, ghost cleanup, drop-position seeding, click suppression), and settings-section state branches. #408: registry-driven eligibility asserted across every section id (URL editor, Host target, Field Editor, Manual controls, Recents, Queue, Settings), summary-backed detach control placement beside the Show/Hide tail, section-surface drag-out with interactive-origin filtering, and Escape cancelling both surface drags and window drags. #398 per-site workspace persistence: layout sanitize/capture/equality helpers, restore gating on the opt-in flag, hydration of detached sections + positions + minimized state, debounced change-only saves, immediate capture on opt-in, reset clearing storage and reattaching, teardown-invalidated restores, and the settings-view toggle/reset dispatch contract. #520 adds the reflow strategy matrix, viewport geometry and pointer thresholds, overlay/adapter decision, and salted opaque URL-structure key prototype. #248 verifies the renamed Field Editor title in detached window accessibility labels.

Window/narrow/placeholder/control states with play tests for detach and restore dispatch; panel-layout story for the detached placeholder; minimized window state and a minimize-toggle play test; SettingsDetached (with play assertions) and SettingsDetachedPrivacyMasked panel stories; #398 panel-layout settings group states (workspace restore off/on) with a toggle/reset dispatch play test

Browser-level detach/restore: floating dialog + placeholder, minimize/expand, close-to-restore with focus hand-off, drag-out placement at the drop point, Escape restore, and detached Settings following the gear toggle without duplication; #408 adds heading-surface drag-out placement and Escape mid-drag cancel. #398 adds the reload round-trip: opt in, detach + drag + minimize, reload restores the arrangement at the saved position, and Reset workspace layout reattaches everything. #520 uses disposable host fixtures to prove overlay cleanup and expose root-inset responsive/fixed failures, wrapper direct-child breakage, transform/layout divergence, and overlay fallback across responsive, fixed/sticky, feed, nested-scroll, iframe, transformed-root, fullscreen, RTL, SPA, and Shadow DOM layouts.

entries.acceptance: In-panel Help surface for shortcuts, features, and settings (issue #352)

coverage.notes: Covers the header Help toggle: opening shows the shared shortcut reference (panel and browser groups) and the feature guide; closing removes the section without trapping focus.

Covers help/toggle reduction, the shared shortcut registry rendering (labels come from the same source the keyboard router uses), feature-guide completeness for the major panel areas, and the privacy-inert static-copy guarantee (no URL or blob values in Help content).

entries.acceptance: User-controlled searchable metadata privacy policy (issue #451) — Slice 1: policy framework, settings surface, and opt-in URL-derived enforcement for new records

coverage.notes: Covers the policy model (plaintext default preserving current behaviour, URL index-key derivation, guards, local-settings sanitize), the durable store keeping the raw URL index under the default while an opt-in encrypted policy hashes only NEW records yet stays findable/dedupable by real URL, dual-encoding lookup resolving a legacy plaintext row after switching to encrypted without ever rewriting existing records, and the Searchable metadata settings control dispatching the updated policy.

Album-name encryption (Slice 2) and the thumbnail class + full field-inventory doc/ADR (Slice 3) land in follow-up slices of this issue; Playwright coverage of the URL policy toggle is added when the setting surface is exercised end-to-end.

Covers stable Recents timestamp order, Queue queueUpdatedAt front/back display paging across the full durable list, one-line collapse headers with static sort controls and persistent bulk-action rows, preference migration, privacy-inert static control labels, and Front/Back pager behavior without changing Recall's canonical order.

coverage.reason: With mixed Queue rows, switch Front first and Back first, page through both directions, open Recall, and confirm Recall remains front-first while neither mode exposes private row metadata.

entries.acceptance: Automatic Single image, Gallery page, and Feed detection with capability-aware per-host override (issue #517)

coverage.notes: Covers the capability model, semantic feed heuristics, gallery and single-image fallbacks, bounded normalized extension-owned override persistence, stale override behavior, action routing, React switcher accessibility, context-aware target labels and Grab hints, and mount lifecycle cleanup.

Covers packaged-extension auto-detection across single, gallery, and semantic-feed fixtures; disabled unsupported modes; context-aware target and Controls behavior; explicit override/reset; per-host persistence across reload; and deterministic artifacts for handoff screenshots 08 and 09.

coverage.reason: Detection quality and the fixed page-level switcher must be checked against representative real-world gallery and infinite-feed markup.
coverage.notes: Open the built extension on one standalone image, one multi-image gallery, and one infinite feed. Verify the automatic label, enabled overrides, target badge, Grab availability and hint, reset-to-automatic behavior, reload persistence, SPA transitions, keyboard focus, and pixel fidelity against handoff screenshots 08 and 09 without exposing URL evidence in the switcher.

entries.acceptance: Accessible Dashboard, Gallery, Recall, and Settings dock with one in-panel route source of truth (issue #516)

coverage.notes: Covers the typed four-route registry, real-tab eligibility for every destination, single activeDestination state, reselect/close/help/settings/minimize/panel-close transitions, preserved primary state, Recall selection continuity across routes plus cleanup on close, producer paging, plain/modifier dock actions, detached Settings single-mount behavior, focus/scroll restoration, and targeted Recall body refresh without replacing destination chrome.

Covers Dashboard, Gallery, Recall, and Settings destination compositions, active-tile and close chrome assertions, populated/empty/loading/error/selected Recall states, and narrow destination geometry.

Covers packaged plain-click routing, keyboard activation and return focus, per-route scroll, minimize/expand, panel close/reopen, detached controller compatibility, durable Gallery loading without queue reorder, Recall paging and selection, explicit and modifier-key real destination tabs, source-tab return and teardown, narrow layout, and deterministic artifacts for handoff screenshots 02 and 04 through 07.

coverage.reason: Pixel fidelity and slide-down motion over arbitrary host pages require a packaged browser comparison.
coverage.notes: At 924x540 and 360x740, compare all four populated routes against screenshots 04 through 07. Verify the active indicator, header and close chrome, 420px overlay geometry, primary workflow behind the surface, selected/stored-original hierarchy, generic privacy copy, visible keyboard focus, scroll restoration, reduced motion, and no full-panel flicker during Recall refresh.

entries.acceptance: Self-contained Dashboard, Gallery, Recall, and Settings extension-page destinations (issue #518)

coverage.notes: Covers shared route definitions, message validation, supported source binding, focus and missing-source fallback, accessible page shell, durable-only dashboard semantics, stale-request suppression, privacy-masked Recall selection, and extension-owned Settings persistence.
Covers Dashboard, interactive Recall, privacy-masked Recall, Settings persistence, empty, loading, error, narrow, and reduced-motion review states using production React destinations.

Covers real packaged Dashboard, Gallery, Recall, and Settings pages over durable repositories; shared navigation; Recents exclusion; queue-producer Recall; Settings persistence across reload and duplicate tabs; source return, source close, and deterministic handoff artifacts.

coverage.reason: Browser chrome and final visual fidelity against the supplied real-tab references require a packaged-extension inspection.
coverage.notes: Open each destination from the panel at 924x540 and 360x740. Compare the real browser tab against handoff screenshots 02 and 04 through 07; verify no faux browser chrome, source return and source-closed states, keyboard focus, privacy masking, reload and duplicate-tab behavior, reduced motion, no horizontal overflow, and no failed stylesheet requests.

entries.acceptance: Image preview backdrop applies instantly without a transition flash (issue #456)

coverage.notes: Covers neutralizing the selected image's transition while it is darkened so Chrome's standalone image-viewer grey->black background-color transition cannot animate/flash the backdrop (worst in fit/letterboxed mode), and restoring the original transition last on release so it cannot flash on close either. Page/element restore stays verbatim (tests/page-style.test.ts).
